meta.note: Edit this file to add quotes, book pages, letters, challenges or podcasts. No HTML changes needed.

quotes.author: Belal Assaad
quotes.text: Your inner well-being is a sanctuary that no other human being holds the key to. No matter how much your external environment tries to dictate your steps, your clothes, or your choices, remember: they can only touch the surface. Your heart and your peace belong to you. Find your Sabr (patience) in the storm, for true freedom is built from within.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: PHYSIOLOGICAL SIGH PACER TRACK: Force-reset your autonomic nervous system from high alert state back to complete baseline calm using the biological cheat code below. Match your real breathing cycles smoothly with the tactile expanding geometry.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: You are not your thoughts. You are the one observing your thoughts. When frustration washes over you, take a mental step back. View it as a scene in a movie playing out in front of you. It is happening in your awareness, but it does not define who you are.

quotes.author: Quran 94:5–6
quotes.text: 'For indeed, with hardship comes ease.' It is a promise. The heavy chapter you are navigating right now is not permanent. It is merely a single page, not the whole book. Protect your energy, guard your inner peace, and trust that doors will open when the time is right.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: Circadian Alignment Protocol: View early morning light in your eyes. On a clear day, aim for 5-10 minutes. On a cloudy, overcast day, aim for 20-30 minutes. This lights up your melanopsin ganglion cells, initiating an instant cortisol surge to keep you awake and focused, whilst starting a countdown timer for healthy melatonin release exactly 16 hours later.

quotes.author: Belal Assaad
quotes.text: The ultimate standard of maturity is to realize that when people judge or attempt to box you in, they are projecting their own inner chaos, fears, and conditioning. Do not inherit their limitations. Keep your focus anchored upward. Your value was already decreed before you were ever introduced to their expectations.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: The 'Gap and Gain' Framework: Human brains automatically measure progress against an idealized perfect state, sparking frustration. Shift this. Force yourself to measure backwards: contrast your current capability and mental fortitude with where you stood last year. This is where real motivation lives.

quotes.author: Islamic Wisdom
quotes.text: In the quiet space between your breaths, there is a sacred sanctuary. When the noise of external expectations tries to define who you must be, sink deeper into your core. You are a deep, vast ocean, and the restrictions of others are merely temporary surface waves. Protect your water, stand firm, and wait for the calm.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: The Friction Law of Learning: Real neuroplasticity—the literal rewiring of your brain circuits—requires physical tension, frustration, and minor errors. When starting a complex habit or trying to focus under mental friction, remind yourself that the agitating feeling of discomfort is the biological signal that triggers neural adaptation.

quotes.author: Rumi
quotes.text: Do not feel lonely, for the entire universe is inside you. Fall deeply in love with the peace of your soul, and remain there. The wound is the exact location where the Light enters you. Your spirit is entirely free, unbounded, and ready to fly long after the cages of life dissolve.

quotes.author: Belal Assaad
quotes.text: True internal peace means silencing the validation-seeking voice inside you. When you move with pure intention, seeking only the pleasure of your Creator and the alignment of your soul, the negative commentary of creation loses its weight completely. Let them talk, while you remain anchored.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: Dopamine Optimization: Avoid stacking multiple dopamine triggers at once—such as looking at social media while listening to intense music and consuming caffeine simultaneously. Stacking creates an artificially high peak followed by a devastating crash in baseline motivation. Protect your baseline focus.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: Neuroscience Breakthrough: Deep within your brain lies the Anterior Midcingulate Cortex. This structure grows in volume when you force yourself to perform tasks you genuinely do not want to do, yet shrinks when you live in absolute comfort. Every time you do the hard thing anyway, you are literally building a bigger, stronger willpower center.

quotes.author: Islamic Wisdom
quotes.text: Your heart is a vessel; if you fill it with the anxieties of tomorrow and the frustrations of today, there will be no room left for peace. Trust the plan designed for you. Protect your spiritual morning and evening spaces like an unbreachable fort. Everything else is temporary white noise.

quotes.author: Andrew Huberman
quotes.text: Adrenaline does not carry an emotional signal. When you feel a racing heart, sweat, or physical tension, your brain labels it as 'trapped' or 'angry'. Override the label. Tell yourself: 'My body is simply mobilizing fuel and energy.' Change the definition, and you dominate the reaction.

quotes.author: Islamic Wisdom
quotes.text: No wall is high enough to hold a soaring spirit. No rule is tight enough to bind an intelligent mind. Close your eyes at night, let your physical location fade into dark space, and tap into the unbounded sky inside you. You are entirely free.

quotes.author: Sh. Yusuf al-Qaradawi
quotes.text: The basic premise is that everything is permissible. Nothing is prohibited except what has been explicitly declared as haram in a clear and authentic text from God or His Messenger.

quotes.author: Sh. Yusuf al-Qaradawi
quotes.text: To make the lawful prohibited, or the prohibited lawful, is tantamount to claiming partnership with God in His legislative authority.

quotes.author: Sh. Yusuf al-Qaradawi
quotes.text: Islam has blocked all avenues leading to the prohibited. If something is forbidden, then any actions, means, and circumstances that lead to it are also forbidden.

quotes.author: Sh. Yusuf al-Qaradawi
quotes.text: Good intentions do not make the unlawful acceptable. The end does not justify the means in Islamic law.

quotes.author: Sh. Yusuf al-Qaradawi
quotes.text: Doubtful things should be avoided in order to keep one's religion and honor pure and untainted.

quotes.author: Abdul Rahman I. Doi
quotes.text: Shariah is the clear path that leads man to spiritual and moral salvation. It is a complete code of life covering every aspect, from individual rituals to societal governance.

quotes.author: Abdul Rahman I. Doi
quotes.text: The law in Islam is not a static set of rules, but a living guidance meant to uphold justice, mercy, and the overall well-being of humanity.

quotes.author: Abdul Rahman I. Doi
quotes.text: The ultimate goal of Shariah is to protect five fundamental rights: faith, life, intellect, lineage, and property.

quotes.author: Abdul Rahman I. Doi
quotes.text: Justice in Islam is an absolute duty, to be upheld even if it goes against oneself, one's parents, or one's kindred.

quotes.author: Abdul Rahman I. Doi
quotes.text: Islamic law provides a harmonious balance between material progress and spiritual elevation, ensuring neither is sacrificed for the other.

quotes.author: Imran Ahsan Khan Nyazee
quotes.text: The goal of Islamic jurisprudence is to provide a strict and scientific methodology for interpreting divine texts and applying them to new, contemporary situations.

quotes.author: Imran Ahsan Khan Nyazee
quotes.text: The nature of justice in Islam is based on a careful balance between individual rights and the collective interest of society, guided by the overarching purposes of the law.

quotes.author: Imran Ahsan Khan Nyazee
quotes.text: Legal interpretation requires not only deep textual understanding, but also a profound insight into the society and the era in which the law is to be applied.

quotes.author: Imran Ahsan Khan Nyazee
quotes.text: The foundational texts provide the universal principles, while jurisprudence bridges the gap between unchanging divine intent and shifting human realities.

quotes.author: Imran Ahsan Khan Nyazee
quotes.text: A jurist must understand the objectives of the law to prevent a rigid application of the letter from defeating the spirit of justice.

quotes.author: Muhammed Al Ubaidy
quotes.text: Our inner self functions like a mirror; if it is covered with the dust of prejudice and ignorance, it can never reflect the pure light of truth and wisdom.

quotes.author: Muhammed Al Ubaidy
quotes.text: Understanding oneself is the first step toward understanding the world. When you clear your inner mirror, your perception of your surroundings changes.

quotes.author: Muhammed Al Ubaidy
quotes.text: True reflection is not about seeing what we want to see, but about having the courage to look at the face of our own flaws and working to heal them.

quotes.author: Muhammed Al Ubaidy
quotes.text: Silence is the polish of the mirror; it is in quiet contemplation that the deepest truths of the soul become visible.

quotes.author: Muhammed Al Ubaidy
quotes.text: Do not blame the mirror if it shows a fractured image; look instead at what you are presenting to it.

quotes.author: Robert Kegan
quotes.text: Human being is fundamentally a meaning-making activity. We do not just mechanically react to the world; we create the meaning that shapes how we experience it.

quotes.author: Robert Kegan
quotes.text: Psychological development is the continuous process where what was once 'Subject' — that which we are fused with and controlled by — becomes 'Object' — that which we can look at, reflect upon, and master.

quotes.author: Robert Kegan
quotes.text: Growth involves the loss of an old way of understanding the world before the new one has fully formed; it is a natural crisis of disorientation and rebirth.

quotes.author: Robert Kegan
quotes.text: People do not just have ideas; they are embedded in them. A shift in development is not just a change in what we think, but a change in how we think.

quotes.author: Robert Kegan
quotes.text: The evolutionary truce is the delicate balance between our need for connection with others and our need for distinct individual autonomy.

quotes.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
quotes.text: He who has a garden and a library, lacks nothing.

quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius
quotes.text: The soul is dyed the color of its thoughts.

quotes.author: Seneca
quotes.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

quotes.author: Blaise Pascal
quotes.text: All of humanity's problems stem from man's inability to sit quietly in a room alone.

quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau
quotes.text: A man is rich in proportion to the number of things which he can afford to let alone.

quotes.author: Socrates
quotes.text: The secret of happiness, you see, is not found in seeking more, but in developing the capacity to enjoy less.

quotes.author: Albert Einstein
quotes.text: Look deep into nature, and then you will understand everything better.

quotes.author: Aristotle
quotes.text: In all things of nature there is something of the marvelous.

quotes.author: Leonardo da Vinci
quotes.text: Small rooms or dwellings discipline the mind, large ones weaken it.

quotes.author: Epictetus
quotes.text: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.

quotes.author: Epicurus
quotes.text: Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not; remember that what you now have was once among the things you only hoped for.

quotes.author: Heraclitus
quotes.text: There is nothing permanent except change.

quotes.author: Confucius
quotes.text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.

quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
quotes.text: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.

quotes.author: Friedrich Nietzsche
quotes.text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.

quotes.author: Pericles
quotes.text: What you leave behind is not what is engraved in stone monuments, but what is woven into the lives of others.

quotes.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
quotes.text: A room without books is like a body without a soul.

quotes.author: Rumi
quotes.text: Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.

quotes.author: Kahlil Gibran
quotes.text: To understand the heart and mind of a person, look not at what he has already achieved, but at what he aspires to.

quotes.author: Zhuangzi
quotes.text: Happiness is the absence of the striving for happiness.

quotes.author: Plato
quotes.text: The measure of a man is what he does with power.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: Nothing says 'genuine human emotion' quite like remembering to adjust your ring light before weeping on camera.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: I am convinced that some people view the internet not as a public forum, but as a personal diary they have accidentally read aloud to millions.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: My favorite genre of video is someone having a deep existential crisis, but making sure to add a trending audio underneath it for algorithm optimization.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: If there is one thing modern creators excel at, it is surviving a situation they completely fabricated for views.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: There is a terrifying level of confidence required to look at a camera, do absolutely nothing for fifteen seconds, and expect the internet to praise you for it.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: Stop sharing your family drama on TikTok. Go to therapy, or at the very least, a group chat.

quotes.author: Italian Bach
quotes.text: Thank you from the bottom of my organs.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: There is no morality without freedom.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The unconscious can make a fool of you in no time.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The unconscious itself initiates the process of renewal.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The primitive mentality does not invent myths, it experiences them.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Nobody has ever seen an archetype, and nobody has ever seen an atom either.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The meeting of two personalities is like the contact of two chemical substances: if there is any reaction, both are transformed.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The least of things with a meaning is worth more in life than the greatest of things without it.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The shoe that fits one person pinches another; there is no recipe for living that suits all cases.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Who looks outside dreams; who looks inside awakes.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Everything that irritates us about others can lead us to an understanding of ourselves.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Loneliness does not come from having no people about one, but from being unable to communicate the things that seem important to oneself.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: The bigger the crowd, the more negligible the individual.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Consciousness is a precondition of being.

quotes.author: Carl Jung
quotes.text: Reason alone does not suffice.

bookPages.title: The Lawful and the Prohibited in Islam
bookPages.subtitle: Cover Page
bookPages.content:
Oasis Library

The Lawful and the Prohibited in Islam

An Essential Guide

Based on the work of

SH YUSUF QARDAWI

Tap the right margin to start reading →

bookPages.title: Section 1: General Principles
bookPages.subtitle: Principles 1 – 4
bookPages.content:
Section 1: General Principles

The Framework of Halal & Haram

Islam provides a clear framework for what is permissible (Halal) and what is forbidden (Haram), built on foundational rules:

  • 1. Basic Permissibility: Everything created by God is naturally permissible unless there is a clear, authentic text from the Quran or Sunnah that explicitly forbids it.
  • 2. God's Right Alone: No human, ruler, or religious authority has the right to declare something Halal or Haram on their own whim.
  • 3. Prohibiting Halal is Shirk: Elevating human opinions to the level of divine law is a form of associating partners with God.
  • 4. Haram Linked to Harm: God only prohibits things that are inherently harmful, impure, or destructive to individuals or society.

bookPages.title: Section 1: General Principles
bookPages.subtitle: Principles 5 – 8
bookPages.content:
Section 1: General Principles

Divine Mercy & Intent

  • 5. Halal Eliminates Need for Haram: For every forbidden thing, God has provided a clean, permissible alternative (e.g., marriage instead of fornication, clean trade instead of usury).
  • 6. Avenues to Haram are Haram: Anything that directly leads to a forbidden act—whether a means, a justification, or a catalyst—is also considered Haram.
  • 7. Falsely Labeling Haram: Using legal loopholes or changing the name of a forbidden act to make it seem acceptable does not change its reality.
  • 8. Intentions Do Not Justify Haram: A noble goal cannot be achieved through forbidden means. The end does not justify the means in Islamic law.

bookPages.title: Section 1: General Principles
bookPages.subtitle: Principles 9 – 11
bookPages.content:
Section 1: General Principles

Doubts & Extreme Necessities

  • 9. Safeguarding Against Doubts: If something falls into a gray area (Shubhat) where its status is unclear, it is best to avoid it to protect one's faith and honor.
  • 10. Haram is Universal: What is forbidden for one group of people is forbidden for all. There are no double standards or privileged classes in divine law.
  • 11. Necessity Overrules Prohibition: In extreme circumstances where survival is at stake (e.g., starvation), the law relaxes, and a person may consume or do what is normally forbidden strictly to survive.

bookPages.title: Section 2: Personal Life
bookPages.subtitle: Dietary Regulations
bookPages.content:
Section 2: Personal Life

Dietary Laws

  • ✦ Permissible Foods: All wholesome, clean, and nutritious foods (vegetables, fruits, grains, and permissible meats) are Halal.
  • ≈ Prohibited Foods: Meat from animals that died of natural causes, spilled blood, pork, and animals sacrificed to anyone other than God.
  • ✦ Halal Slaughter: Animals must be slaughtered humanely by invoking the name of God and swiftly cutting the jugular veins to ensure quick death and complete drainage of blood.
  • ≈ Intoxicants: Alcohol and all mind-altering substances are strictly Haram, even in small quantities. Buying, selling, or producing them is equally forbidden.

bookPages.title: Section 2: Personal Life
bookPages.subtitle: Clothing & Adornment
bookPages.content:
Section 2: Personal Life

Clothing and Home

  • ✦ Modesty: Clothing must cover the private parts (Awrah) and should not be transparent, excessively tight, or designed to flaunt arrogance.
  • ≈ Gold and Silk: Pure silk clothing and gold jewelry are forbidden for Muslim men but permitted for women.
  • ≈ Extreme Alterations: Permanent modifications to the body driven by vanity (such as tattoos or changing creation for purely cosmetic enhancement) are forbidden, while general hygiene and grooming are highly encouraged.

bookPages.title: Section 3: Marriage & Family
bookPages.subtitle: The Marriage Covenant
bookPages.content:
Section 3: Family Life

The Marriage Contract

  • ✦ The Foundation: Marriage is a sacred covenant aimed at building a peaceful home based on mutual love, respect, and tranquility.
  • ≈ Prohibited Marriages: Incestuous relationships and marriages with polytheists are forbidden.
  • ✦ The Dowry (Mahr): A mandatory gift from the groom to the bride, establishing her financial independence and rights within the marriage.

bookPages.title: Section 3: Marriage & Family
bookPages.subtitle: Spousal Relations
bookPages.content:
Section 3: Family Life

Spousal Relations and Rights

  • ✦ Mutual Kindness: Husbands and wives must treat each other with fairness, kindness, and equity.
  • ≈ Fidelity: Adultery and fornication (Zina) are major sins. Islam blocks paths to Zina by encouraging modesty and regulating free mixing between unrelated genders.
  • ✦ Divorce (Talaq): Allowed only as a last resort when reconciliation is completely impossible. It must be executed with fairness, respect, and zero harm.

bookPages.title: Section 4: Daily Life
bookPages.subtitle: Commerce & Business
bookPages.content:
Section 4: Daily Life & Commerce

Commercial Transactions

  • ✦ Honest Trade: Earning a living through lawful trade, employment, and business contracts is highly virtuous.
  • ≈ Usury & Interest (Riba): Strictly prohibited due to its exploitative nature, which enriches the wealthy at the expense of the vulnerable.
  • ≈ Deception & Gambling: Any transaction involving fraud, hoarding goods to artificially raise prices, bribery, or gambling (Maysir) is Haram.

bookPages.title: Section 4: Daily Life
bookPages.subtitle: Social Etiquette
bookPages.content:
Section 4: Daily Life & Commerce

Social Etiquette & Recreation

  • ✦ Respecting Privacy: Entering homes without permission or spying on others is forbidden.
  • ≈ Brotherhood: Backbiting, slander, spreading rumors, and mocking others are strictly prohibited.
  • ✦ Recreation: Entertainment, sports, and music are permissible as long as they do not promote vulgarity, neglect religious obligations, or involve forbidden elements.

letters.title: Counting Down
letters.text: Danna. I hope Russia is treating you well and you are having a great time. I was just looking at the calendar. June 26th is getting closer every single day. I really can't wait for this waiting period to be over so we can finally take the next step together. I love you.

letters.title: Thinking About Us
letters.text: Hope you are having a beautiful day on your trip today. It is pretty quiet over here without you. I just wanted to drop a little note to remind you how excited I am to marry you. Enjoy your vacation and know that I am right here waiting for you.

letters.title: My Future Wife
letters.text: Just leaving a quick message to make you smile. The iddah feels long sometimes but you are so incredibly worth the wait. Have an amazing time in Russia and remember that you are my absolute favorite person in the whole world.

letters.title: Missing You
letters.text: I hope you are getting some good rest and exploring a lot today. I miss you a ton. Every day that passes brings us closer to being together properly and starting our life. Take care of yourself out there. I love you so much.

letters.title: Almost There
letters.text: Just a quick reminder that the 26th is right around the corner. Go have fun in Russia and enjoy the rest of your vacation to the fullest. We have a whole lifetime together waiting for us very soon. Miss you endlessly.

microChallenges: Relax your shoulders, slightly part your teeth, and let your jaw completely unclench. Notice the weight of your body on your seat.
Trace the cold temperature of a glass of water all the way down to your stomach during your next sip.
Silently count backwards from 10 to 1 whenever you feel the urge to react to an annoying or controlling remark.
Look at a shadow cast in your room right now. Map its exact outline, gradients, and boundaries with cold focus.
Give someone an incredibly polite, serene smile that completely masks your real, secret strategic goals.
Sit in absolute stillness for exactly 60 seconds. Do nothing but trace the physical sound of your own quiet inhales.

groundingSteps.inst: Look around you and locate 5 visible objects. Pay close attention to their shadows, edges, texture, or colors.
Acknowledge 4 things you can physically feel. The weight of your phone, your feet on the rug, your hair, or the air on your neck.
Listen closely and identify 3 distinct sounds. It could be faraway traffic, a bird, the sound of your own breathing, or the wind.
Notice 2 smells around you. Your clothes, your soap, a book page, or simply the scent of the room you are in.
Acknowledge 1 thing you can taste, or simply notice the neutral taste in your mouth. Sip some cold water if you can.
